title: Delicate Crisp Japanese Mung Bean Sprouts Tendon
description: A light and crispy Japanese dish featuring mung bean sprouts, perfect for a weeknight dinner in the Chubu region, this recipe is egg-free-friendly and utilizes a brine-wet method to enhance flavor and texture.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine water and salt to create a brine, stirring until the salt is dissolved.
Add the mung bean sprouts to the brine and let them soak for 30 minutes.
Drain and rinse the mung bean sprouts with cold water, then set them aside.
In a small saucepan, combine rice vinegar, sugar, and 2 tablespoons of water, bringing the mixture to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce for 5 minutes, or until it thickens slightly.
In a large skillet or wok, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Add the mung bean sprouts to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until they are crispy and golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the sesame oil, grated ginger, and cooked mung bean sprouts to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Pour the sauce into the skillet and stir to coat the mung bean sprouts.
Transfer the mung bean sprouts to a serving platter and garnish with chopped green onions.
